task-memory-reference.purpose: Preserve aligned goal, slice, diagram, and memory context for long Compass-guided work when LLM context may be lost or compressed.

activation.rule: Do not create task memory for every task.
Create task memory only for long or risky multi-slice work.
The task type or discovered risk must suggest long-running work, such as new_feature, architecture_change, large refactor, or another task with meaningful checkpoint risk.
Compass must have at least two concrete implementation slices after align-context or fit-design.
Create task memory after the developer and agent have aligned on the goal and slices, and before the first implementation slice starts.
If the task has only one implementation slice, do not create task memory unless the developer explicitly asks for it.

goal-statuses.status: The goal is currently in progress.
The goal is finished and verified.
The goal was replaced by a new goal.
The goal stopped without a replacement.

slice-statuses.status: The slice is planned but not started.
The slice is currently being worked on.
The slice is complete and verified.
The slice cannot proceed until a blocker is resolved.

file.name: goal.md
file.purpose: Source of truth for goal identity, status, description, non-goals, success criteria, slice list, links to superseded or successor goals, and latest evidence.
file.rule: Update goal.md whenever the goal status, slice list, slice status, blocker, successor link, or latest evidence changes.

file.name: diagram.md
file.purpose: Human-readable checkpoint visualization using Mermaid plus text support.
file.rule: Include status legend and a text fallback so the checkpoint map remains readable when Mermaid is not rendered.
Update the Mermaid diagram whenever goal.md or memories.md changes slice or goal state.

file.name: memories.md
file.purpose: Summaries and reverse-chronological histories for context recovery.
file.rule: Keep SUMMARIES current with the latest compressed state.
The LLM memikirkan field must contain a rationale snapshot, not private chain-of-thought.

lifecycle.event: Create all required files after align-context or fit-design and before the first implementation slice.
Mark the slice active in goal.md, update diagram.md to blue, and add a memory history entry.
Mark the slice done in goal.md, record evidence, update diagram.md to green, and add a memory history entry.
Mark the slice blocked in goal.md, record the blocker, update diagram.md to red, and add a memory history entry.
If the goal remains stable, update the existing task folder instead of creating a new folder.
Create a new task folder, mark the old goal superseded, cross-link both goals, and record the reason in both memories.md files.
Mark the goal completed and record final evidence.

resume.rule: At the start of a long Compass-guided task, inspect docs/.tasks for active relevant goals before creating a new task memory folder.
If exactly one active relevant goal exists, load goal.md, diagram.md, and memories.md before planning or implementation.
If multiple active goals could match, ask one clarification question before choosing.
If an active goal conflicts with the user's new request, treat that as a potential goal change and ask before superseding it.

mermaid.rule: Use flowchart diagrams for checkpoint flow unless the user asks for another Mermaid type.
Use class definitions for pending, active, done, and blocked states.
Use short node labels and explain detail in surrounding text.
